displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #3: Homecoming
synopsis:
Searching an abandoned city on Rhen Var, Vima Sunrider finds Ulic Qel-Droma, who at first mistakes her for her mother Nomi. She begs him to train her, but Ulic refuses, insisting he is no longer a Jedi and can no longer feel the Force, and sends her away. When she is caught in a blizzard, he takes pity and shelters her.

On Cathar, Sylvar is welcomed home as a hero but finds no peace, and the holy man Kharr tells her that her true enemy is the anger within. She sets out on a blood hunt to purge her rage, leading Tott Doneeta into a cave to slaughter a Kiltik queen and her brood; horrified, Tott warns that her fury leads to the dark side. On Rhen Var, Ulic confesses to Vima the full cost of the Jedi path, the losses and betrayals he has suffered, and asks why she still wants him. She answers simply, because he is Ulic. Elsewhere, Hoggon realizes he has been ferrying the wanted criminal Ulic and resolves to warn Nomi at Exis Station, where she learns her daughter intends to be trained by him.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #3: Homecoming
